journey.title: You don't need more skills. You need to see what you already have.
journey.subtitle: Put your fragments together — Me helps you discover directions you never thought of.
journey.selfDesc: Drop in your fragments. Me shows you directions they point to. Not fortune-telling — just laying out what you already have.
journey.selfCta: See what my fragments can build
journey.coCreateReady1: Your fragments are enough to paint a self-portrait. Want to find someone to see what you two can build together?
journey.coCreateReady2: Some directions need another pair of eyes. Maybe worth co-creating.
journey.coCreateReady3: When you're ready to walk with someone, two journeys can meet here.

processValue.title: You don't have to wait until it's 'complete' to see the direction
processValue.desc: Every fusion gives you a concrete next step. Not motivational quotes — something you can do today.
processValue.quote: Teachers don't think they can sell courses, delivery riders don't think they can make videos — Me helps you see what you already possess.

different.title: AI is everywhere. What's missing is someone who remembers your fragments.
different.subtitle: Generic AI forgets after each chat. Me remembers every fragment — the more you add, the sharper it gets.
different.compareFragmentsMe: ✅ Fragments saved permanently, grow over time
different.compareStartupMe: ✅ Pick a profession, get preset fragments

teacher.title: Teacher Wang didn't learn anything new — her monthly income increased by ¥4000
teacher.subtitle: She just pieced together what she was already doing every day.
teacher.persona: Wang, 38, middle school Chinese teacher in a third-tier city, 12 years teaching
teacher.description: Monthly salary ¥4500, 2 months free in summer, thinks this is all there is
teacher.tagline: The skills you use every day might be what others will pay for.
teacher.tagline2: No new skills needed. What you have is enough.
teacher.beforeItem1: Repeating lesson prep, teaching, grading — feels her skills are worthless
teacher.beforeItem2: Posted reading notes on WeChat, got likes but no one paid
teacher.beforeItem3: Thinks: I just know how to teach — what can I possibly sell?

fragments.text: Curriculum design (12 years of lesson plans)
PPT creation (colleagues always borrow her templates)
Patient listening (parents love chatting with her)
Tutoring neighbor's kid: essay score improved 15 points

teacher.quote: You've always known how to teach — you just haven't learned to price yourself yet.
teacher.direction: Summer Essay Training Camp + Reading Notes IP + Parent Communication Consulting
evidence: You have curriculum design skills: a '7-Day Essay Mastery' course is absolutely doable
You have public speaking experience: recording online courses is no problem
You have a reading notes habit: natural content for Xiaohongshu/WeChat Official Account
You have patient listening: parents will pay for consulting — you're more effective than a psychologist
actions: This week: Post your best 5 reading notes to Xiaohongshu, test the traffic
Next week: Use your PPT template to draft a '7-Day Essay Mastery' outline
Before summer: Post recruitment for 'Essay Training Camp' on Xiaohongshu, price at ¥299/person
results: First month: 20 reading notes posted, gained 800 followers
Summer: first session of Essay Training Camp, 8 students, ¥2392 revenue
teacher.footnote: Using exactly what she was doing every day — teaching, reading, chatting
teacher.tagline: The skills you use every day might be what others will pay for.
teacher.tagline2: No new skills needed. What you have is enough.

rider.title: 🛵 Delivery Rider Xiao Li
rider.quote: Every delivery you run is content nobody else can film.
rider.direction: Delivery-Worker Food Reviews + Weird Order Story Series
rider.fragmentsDesc: Knows city routes + Can fix e-bikes + Watches short videos 3h/day + Weird order stories

moreCases.next: The next one could be you

steps.title: How does it work?
steps.subtitle: A four-step loop. Each step is simple — the hard part was never seeing what you had.
steps.step1Desc: What you can do, what you love, what people always ask your help with — write it down. AI auto-categorizes.
steps.step2Desc: Put your fragments together. Me tells you what directions they can form. Not fluff — concrete suggestions.
steps.step3Desc: Every direction comes with a specific action you can do today. Not lectures — real, actionable steps.
steps.step4Desc: Come back with results. New fragments come in. Next fusion gets sharper.

map.title: See exactly where you are on the journey
map.subtitle: Every direction generates a journey map. Not empty promises — every step is labeled.
map.progressTip: Tap any node to see step details, drag to adjust progress

features.title: Landmarks
features.desc: Each step is a landmark with a unique icon

features.title: Walking Character
features.desc: See yourself move through each step in real time
